ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Bo Luo Bao (Hong Kong pineapple bun); mapped to the closest parent class 'sweet bun' (FOODON_03315021) as an approximate match.
ontology.foodex2_description: Bread and similar products — sweet bun type
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Bo Luo Bao; A000E (bread and similar products) used as the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific baked good; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Bo Luo Bao bun weighs approximately 80 g, which is a typical single-serve portion as sold in Hong Kong-style bakeries and cha chaan teng establishments in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Bo Luo Bao (Pineapple Bun) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists Bo Luo Bao as a named entry with per-100g nutrient values. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken directly from this source. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Sweet roll, commercially prepared' (FDC ID 168880) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous food: white bread / sweet enriched bun
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bo Luo Bao in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 72 is estimated from the closest analogous food: white bread / enriched sweet bun (GI ~70–75, University of Sydney GI Database). The high sugar content (13.3 g/100g), low fibre (1.3 g/100g), and refined wheat flour base support a high-GI classification. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (72 × 39.8) / 100 ≈ 28.7 for an 80 g serving (available carbs = 49.9 × 0.80 − 1.3 × 0.80 = 38.9 g ≈ 39.8 g net).

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (13.3 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.3 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 294 mg/100g adds direct vascular load
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should limit Bo Luo Bao to occasional small portions, as the combination of 294 mg sodium, 13.3 g sugar, and high saturated fat per 100 g collectively elevates blood pressure through sodium retention, arterial stiffness, and insulin-driven fluid dysregulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (49.9 g/100g) and high sugar (13.3 g/100g) with very low dietary fibre (1.3 g/100g) produce a high glycemic response (estimated GI 72)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Bo Luo Bao due to its high estimated GI of 72, high sugar load, and minimal fibre, all of which cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, pair with a protein source and restrict to a quarter-bun portion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g exceeds the 5 g/100g threshold; trace trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) from butter and shortening further raises LDL cholesterol and lowers HDL
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of Bo Luo Bao, as its high saturated fat content (~9.5 g/100g) and trace trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) from the cookie crust contribute to atherogenic dyslipidaemia and increased cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100g and trans fat ~0.2 g/100g are pro-inflammatory; high refined sugar (13.3 g/100g) further promotes inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Bo Luo Bao due to its pro-inflammatory saturated fat and trans fat content from the butter-shortening crust, combined with high refined sugar that amplifies systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bo Luo Bao (菠蘿包 / 菠萝包), literally 'pineapple bun', is a quintessential Hong Kong-style sweet bun widely consumed across Hong Kong, Macau, and in Cantonese communities throughout Southeast Asia including Singapore. Despite its name, the bun contains no pineapple; the name derives from the distinctive criss-cross pattern on its golden, crispy sugar-and-fat cookie crust, which visually resembles the skin of a pineapple. The interior is a soft, lightly sweetened enriched yeast dough made from wheat flour, eggs, milk, butter, and sugar. The crust is made from a separate shortcrust-style mixture of butter or lard, sugar, egg, and flour, piped or pressed onto the top of the bun before baking. The bun is baked until the crust is golden and crackled. A popular variant, 'bo lo yau' (菠蘿油), is served split open with a thick slab of cold butter inserted inside. Nutritionally, Bo Luo Bao is energy-dense at 419 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (20.6 g), high refined carbohydrate (49.9 g), significant sugar (13.3 g), and low dietary fibre (1.3 g). Its estimated glycemic index of 72 places it in the high-GI category, making it unsuitable for blood glucose management. Saturated fat is estimated at approximately 9.5 g/100g from the butter and shortening in the crust, and trace trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) may be present. Sodium is 294 mg/100g. Clinically, this food is of high concern for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease due to its glycemic load, saturated fat, and sodium profile. It is commonly eaten as a breakfast item or afternoon snack in cha chaan teng (Hong Kong-style cafes) and bakeries.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a pineapple bun?
Can diabetics eat bo luo bao?
What is the GI of pineapple bun?
Is bo luo bao suitable for hypertension?
What is the difference between bo luo bao and bo lo yau?
How much fat is in a Hong Kong pineapple bun?

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database, which is the primary approved source for Singapore-consumed foods. Saturated fat is estimated from the closest USDA FoodData Central analogue (sweet roll, commercially prepared) and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white bread / enriched sweet bun as the reference, which is defensible given the food's composition.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.5 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry 'Sweet roll, commercially prepared' (FDC ID 168880) as HPB Foodo does not separately report saturated fat for Bo Luo Bao. This is an approved secondary source used for a field not covered by the primary source. 2. Trans fat (0.2 g/100g) is an estimate based on the known use of butter and shortening/lard in the cookie crust; no direct measurement is available from HPB or USDA for this specific item. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 as these values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for Bo Luo Bao and are not available in the USDA FoodData Central closest analogue at the required specificity for this preparation. 4. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Bo Luo Bao was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16. 5. Halal status is marked false as traditional recipes may use lard in the crust; halal-certified variants exist but cannot be assumed for the generic record. 6. Bo Luo Bao is a Hong Kong-origin food widely consumed in Singapore; multilingual names are provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per the Singaporean dish multilingual rule, as this food is a staple in Singapore's Cantonese bakery and cha chaan teng culture.
